message:
test(codec): conformance harness for PDU wire format

Adds tests/conformance_pdu_wire.rs with 15 tests pinning the decoder's
response to the full boundary matrix:

- minimal valid (Ping round-trip)
- zero-length payload (empty ErrorResponse reason)
- one-byte payload
- 1 MB payload round-trip (well below cap, still substantial)
- MAX_PDU_SIZE + 1 rejection — crafted frame claims > 256 MB data_len,
  decoder's size check fires BEFORE the vec![0u8; data_len] allocation
  that would OOM the process
- empty buffer → stream_decode None
- partial leb128 continuation byte → stream_decode None (whole-frame-
  or-nothing contract)
- length present, payload missing → stream_decode None
- trailing garbage → first frame decodes, extra bytes stay in buffer
- two back-to-back PDUs → both decode cleanly, buffer drains
- non-canonical leb128 with impossible header arithmetic →
  "sizes don't make sense" error (pins the sanity-check path)
- unknown ident with zero data → Pdu::Invalid{ident}, serial preserved
- whole-frame-or-nothing: feed the buffer one byte at a time and assert
  that every proper prefix decodes to None without mutating the buffer,
  then the final byte triggers exactly one decode
- compressed-flag high-bit round-trip via a 260 KB repetitive payload
  that auto-triggers zstd, with a >2x shrink assertion
- constants guard: asserts the encoder's output bit 63 stays clear on
  small Ping frames so COMPRESSED_MASK drift fails loudly before the
  rest of the suite can silently misinterpret compressed frames

Wire format under test (from encode_raw):

  tagged_len : leb128 u64   (bit 63 = zstd-compressed)
  serial     : leb128 u64
  ident      : leb128 u64
  data       : tagged_len - enc_len(serial) - enc_len(ident) bytes

Frames are hand-built via a leb128_u64 + frame() helper pair so every
byte on the wire is under test control. MAX_PDU_SIZE (256 MB) and
COMPRESSED_MASK (1<<63) are mirrored from lib.rs private constants;
the constants-guard test is the tripwire if either drifts.

Run: cargo test -p codec --test conformance_pdu_wire \
     --no-default-features --features async-asupersync

15 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored in 3m 34s cold build on a fresh
CARGO_TARGET_DIR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
